title: gypsophila hirsuta var. thymifolia
intro: Gypsophila hirsuta var. thymifolia, also known as woolly gypsophila, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its woolly stems and white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of gypsophila hirsuta var. thymifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of gypsophila hirsuta var. thymifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
